# Dev Journey — Full Stack Web Development Classes

> Dev Journey is a free, beginner-friendly full stack web development course by
> Chebon Kelvin Kibet, a freelance full stack engineer. It teaches JavaScript,
> React, Next.js, Node.js and PostgreSQL step by step using playful, real-world
> analogies and runnable code examples, aimed at complete beginners.

Website: https://fullstackclassesforchebon.web.app
Author: Chebon Kelvin Kibet — https://chebonkelvin.com
Contact: contact@chebonkelvin.com

## Courses

- [JavaScript Tutorial](https://fullstackclassesforchebon.web.app/javascript.html):
  Variables, data types, operators, string methods, template literals, if/else,
  switch statements, loops, functions, arrow functions, arrays and array methods.

- [React Tutorial](https://fullstackclassesforchebon.web.app/react.html):
  What React is, JSX, functional components, props, useState, useEffect,
  lists & keys, conditional rendering, forms, useContext, useRef,
  useMemo and useCallback.

- [Next.js Tutorial](https://fullstackclassesforchebon.web.app/nextjs.html):
  What Next.js is, file-based routing, pages & layouts, server vs client
  components, data fetching (SSR), static generation (SSG), API routes,
  dynamic routes & params, image optimization and metadata.

- [Node.js Tutorial](https://fullstackclassesforchebon.web.app/nodejs.html):
  What Node.js is, modules, file system, HTTP servers, Express.js, routing,
  middleware, REST APIs, JWT authentication, environment variables, CORS
  and error handling.

- [PostgreSQL Tutorial](https://fullstackclassesforchebon.web.app/postgres.html):
  What PostgreSQL is, hosting on Railway, tables & columns, data types,
  INSERT, SELECT, WHERE, UPDATE, DELETE, primary & foreign keys, JOINs,
  GROUP BY and aggregates.

## Teaching Style

Every lesson explains one concept with a simple everyday analogy (e.g. variables
as "magic boxes", middleware as "assembly line workers"), followed by real code
examples. Lessons are ordered so each builds on the previous one, forming a
complete beginner-to-full-stack learning path.

## Recommended Learning Order

1. JavaScript (language fundamentals)
2. React (frontend UI)
3. Next.js (fullstack React framework)
4. Node.js (backend & APIs)
5. PostgreSQL (databases & SQL)
